summary: High‑energy 1993 show with stellar Jerry solos and a solid soundboard capture, highlighted by Minglewood Blues, So Many Roads and an epic Other One > Morning Dew.
review: Fans rave about the fire‑filled second set – from a screaming Minglewood Blues through a scorching So Many Roads, a majestic Friend of the Devil, and a monster Other One → Morning Dew that ends with an emotional Jerry solo. The soundboard source is described as clean and listenable, letting the tight bandwork (especially Vince’s keyboards and Phil’s bass) shine throughout.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars and praise both performance and recording; only one joke review is negative, so the combined weighted score is strong.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No specific complaints about clarity or mix; reviewers focus on musical aspects, implying a clear and listenable soundboard capture.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Two‑set show with a fun, passionate first set and a deep, jam‑rich second set; transitions (Mexicali > Big River, Wave to the Wind > Terrapin) are praised for tightness.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers highlight his soulful solos on So Many Roads, Riders, Other One and an emotional closing Morning Dew.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for “killing it” on bass during The Other One and for loving the deep jams; overall tight rhythm section noted.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince Welnick receives strong praise (especially on Terrapin) and Bruce’s grand‑piano contribution is called superlative.
